answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: SQL stands for Structured Query Language. It is used to communicate with databases.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Remember: Q means Query, not Question.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Use SELECT * FROM students; to display all columns and all rows from the students table.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Use SELECT name, age FROM students; when you need only the name and age columns.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Write column names separated by commas.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Use WHERE to filter rows. SELECT * FROM students WHERE city = 'Lucknow'; returns only students from Lucknow.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: Text values like Lucknow should be written inside quotes.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Use ORDER BY to sort records. SELECT * FROM students ORDER BY age ASC; shows students from younger to older.

answers.author: Champak Roy
answers.content: Use DISTINCT to show unique values. SELECT DISTINCT city FROM students; shows each city only once.

answers.author: System Hint
answers.content: DISTINCT removes duplicate values from the result.
